title: Horizontal Scroll
subtitle: {key:zs}, {key:ze}, {key:zh}, {key:zl} — scroll left/right when wrap is off.
summary: When 'wrap' is off, long lines run off the screen. Horizontal scrolling commands let you see the rest. zs scrolls so the cursor is at the left edge; ze at the right.

blocks.text: Long single-line content (logs, JSON, CSV) runs off the right edge when {key:'wrap'} is off. The {key:z}-family includes a horizontal scroll group that mirrors the vertical one.

blocks.label: Scroll horizontally so cursor is at left edge
Scroll so cursor is at right edge
Scroll viewport right one column (text appears to move left)
Scroll viewport left one column (text appears to move right)
Scroll viewport right half a screen
Scroll viewport left half a screen

blocks.title: Only Useful When 'wrap' Is Off
blocks.text:
By default, Vim wraps long lines (`:set wrap`). In wrap mode, horizontal scroll is meaningless — every character is visible somewhere on a continuation line.

Set `:set nowrap` for working with logs, CSV, JSON, etc. — then horizontal scroll matters. {key:z}{key:s} (start) and {key:z}{key:e} (end) reposition the viewport so the cursor sits at the left or right edge. {key:z}{key:l}/{key:z}{key:h} are one-column nudges; {key:z}{key:L}/{key:z}{key:H} are half-screen jumps.

`'sidescroll'` controls the step size for automatic horizontal scrolling when the cursor moves off-screen. `'sidescrolloff'` is the horizontal counterpart to `'scrolloff'`.
